sentence_templates: {name} stood unyielding as a {archetype}, shield set against the dark.
{name} moved through the shadows as a {archetype}, counting exits before enemies.
{name} danced between death and fortune as a {archetype}.
{name} channeled forbidden energies deep into the ruin.
They carved a path through the prison stones to floor {floor}.
They reached the crypt depths, where every door remembered a burial.
They descended toward the magma heart, where the walls breathed heat.
In the end, even vermin found the gap in their guard.
A goblin's crude blade wrote the final line.
Orcish strength finished what the dungeon began.
A wraith took the warmth from their last breath.
The dungeon itself struck from below, patient and exact.
Only {kills} foes fell before the silence closed in.
They cut a clean swath through {kills} foes.
They left a trail of {kills} corpses behind them.
The Vampire Fang pulsed at their belt, hungry even at the end.
The Glass Cannon's promise finally found its price.
Phoenix Ash scattered over the wound, but not enough remained to rise.
The Leech Vampire's power beat with every kill until the dungeon found a deeper vein.
Shadow Dance carried them between blades, but not beyond fate.
They died one floor before a reckoning that would have named them legend.
Even {best_item} could not buy another dawn.
Few Echoes followed them home: {echo_count}, dim but not silent.
The dungeon returned {echo_count} Echoes, each one a lesson.
The prison was behind them, broken locks and colder cells left in their wake.
The crypt yielded, but not without whispering their name.
Fire took the final argument from their lungs.
Poison waited until courage had nowhere left to go.
The Stone Guardian ended them like a door closing forever.
The Shadow Wraith unmade their shape before taking their life.
The Magma Titan burned their ambition down to glass.
They found a safe floor once, and learned safety is never a promise.
They carried little gold, but enough scars to pay the ferryman.
Gold weighed their pack down, but could not hold death back.
Their best blade, {best_item}, was still warm when it fell from their hand.
A scroll remained unread, its ink still bright with impossible routes.
Their story ended on floor {floor}, but the dungeon remembered the shape of it.
They killed no one, and the dungeon showed no mercy for restraint.
Few reach so deep; fewer still return with a voice.
So ended {name}: floor {floor}, {kills} kills, slain by {cause}.
